# WebWaka OS — Verticals Execution Prompts: Commerce P2 Batch 1

**Document type:** Agent execution prompt set
**Scope:** Commerce P2 verticals (Batch 1) — 9 verticals covered
**Repo:** https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os
**Branch base:** `main`
**Milestone targets:** M9–M10
**Status:** Ready for agent execution — all P1 verticals complete (M8b–M8e)
**Generated by:** MASTER_CONTINUATION_PROMPT.md — Set A

---

> **3-in-1 Platform Note:**
> Every vertical in this document serves at least **Pillar 1 (Ops)** and **Pillar 3 (Marketplace/Discovery)**.
> Verticals marked with Pillar 2 also activate `apps/brand-runtime/` (branded site/storefront).
> **SuperAgent AI is cross-cutting — it is NOT a fourth pillar.** All AI features route through `packages/superagent`.
> See `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md` for the full pillar map and `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` for per-vertical pillar classification.

### General rules for all agents using these prompts

- Never make assumptions about WebWaka's architecture, API contracts, or business logic. Always read the referenced documents and code first.
- Research deeply before executing. When encountering domain-specific patterns, do online research first and synthesise with Nigeria market context.
- Thoroughness is far more important than speed.
- All work must be pushed to GitHub. No local partial work should remain outside the repo.
- SuperAgent is the AI layer — all AI features in verticals route through `packages/superagent`. Never call AI providers directly from vertical code.
- Platform Invariants are non-negotiable. Read `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` in full before implementing any vertical.
- The verticals FSM engine in `packages/verticals/` governs lifecycle states. All verticals must use it — no custom state machines.
- Every monetary value stored in the database must be an integer in kobo (P9). No decimals, no floats.
- No cross-tenant data leakage (T3). Every D1 query scopes to `tenant_id`.

---

## TASK V-COMM-EXT-A1: Auto Mechanic / Garage Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `auto-mechanic`
- **Priority:** P2-Top100 — High Nigeria market density; millions of roadside mechanics and garages across all states
- **Milestone target:** M9
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 2 (Branding) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","branding","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:**
  - Verticals master plan: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md
  - 3-in-1 platform architecture: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md
  - Verticals FSM engine: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/packages/verticals/src/
  - Verticals seed: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv
  - Platform invariants: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/platform-invariants.md
  - Entitlement model: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/entitlement-model.md
  - AI integration framework: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md
  - SuperAgent product spec: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/superagent/02-product-spec.md

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in automotive services management software and multi-tenant SaaS platforms, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian automotive/repair sector operations — job cards, parts sourcing from Ladipo Market (Lagos) / Nnewi (Anambra), roadworthiness compliance
- VIO (Vehicle Inspection Officers) and SON (Standards Organisation of Nigeria) regulatory integration
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode
- WebWaka SuperAgent integration — all AI features route through `packages/superagent`
- Verticals FSM lifecycle engine — extend base FSM, never replace it
- Nigerian regulatory context: SON (standards compliance), VIO (roadworthiness), state transport ministry

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

Read and fully internalize ALL of the following before writing any code:

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — Commerce verticals section; entry for `auto-mechanic`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md` — Pillar 1/2/3 classification; how branding and marketplace pillars activate
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4 in full
- `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv` — row for `auto-mechanic`: FSM states, KYC tier, required dependencies
- `packages/verticals/src/` — FSM engine API: `registerVertical()`, `transition()`, `getVerticalState()`
- `packages/auth/src/guards.ts` — `requireKYCTier()`, `requireAiRights()`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md` — how verticals call SuperAgent; WakaCU burn pattern
- `docs/governance/entitlement-model.md` — which plan tiers unlock which capabilities
- `docs/governance/ai-capability-matrix.md` — AI capabilities available per tier
- `infra/db/migrations/` — check last migration number before assigning new number (currently 0056 is last)

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Act as a **Senior Product Researcher** with deep Nigerian SME market expertise.

Research tasks (search online before writing the plan):
- Nigerian auto mechanic / garage sector market size — SMEDAN data, number of registered workshops across Lagos, Abuja, Port Harcourt, Kano, Nnewi auto clusters
- Top 10 Nigerian and African competitors in workshop management software (Autochek Africa, CarDash, FixMyCar NG, Autofix, GarageHive Africa, etc.)
- Features these competitors offer that matter most to Nigerian mechanics — job card tracking, parts inventory from Ladipo/Nnewi, customer SMS notifications, vehicle history, payment receipts
- VIO roadworthiness certificate workflow and SON compliance for auto service businesses
- Common operational pain points: no digital job cards, cash-only, no parts inventory, no customer follow-up

Execution plan must specify:
- **Objective:** Give every Nigerian auto mechanic or garage a digital back office — job cards, parts inventory, customer vehicle history, service invoicing in kobo, and AI-powered demand forecasting
- **Schema additions:**
  - Table: `auto_mechanic_profiles` — workshop name, CAC number, VIO registration, state, LGA, workspace_id, tenant_id
  - Table: `job_cards` — vehicle plate, customer phone, complaint description, diagnosis, mechanic_id, labour_cost_kobo, status (open/in_progress/completed/invoiced), tenant_id
  - Table: `parts_inventory` — part name, part number, quantity_in_stock, unit_cost_kobo, supplier, tenant_id
  - Migration: assign next number after 0056 (check `infra/db/migrations/`)
- **API routes:** `POST /v1/verticals/auto-mechanic/register`, `GET /v1/verticals/auto-mechanic/:id`, `POST /v1/verticals/auto-mechanic/:id/transition`, `POST /v1/verticals/auto-mechanic/:id/job-cards`, `GET /v1/verticals/auto-mechanic/:id/job-cards`, `POST /v1/verticals/auto-mechanic/:id/parts`, `POST /v1/verticals/auto-mechanic/:id/ai-advisory`
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `cac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent integration point:** `DEMAND_PLANNING` (parts reorder forecasting) + `SALES_FORECAST` (service revenue trend) — gated by `aiRights`, Growth plan minimum, NDPR consent required
- **Regulatory hook:** VIO registration number captured at `cac_verified` state; SON compliance flag stored on profile
- **KYC tier gate:** Tier 2 (BVN-linked) for labour invoicing above ₦50,000/day; Tier 1 for basic job card and receipts

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-auto-mechanic` from `main`.

**3a. Schema migration:**

Create `infra/db/migrations/[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_auto_mechanic.sql`:
```sql
CREATE TABLE auto_mechanic_profiles (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  workspace_id TEXT REFERENCES workspaces(id),
  tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  workshop_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  cac_number TEXT,
  vio_registration TEXT,
  state TEXT NOT NULL,
  lga TEXT NOT NULL,
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'seeded',
  created_at INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch()),
  updated_at INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
);
CREATE INDEX idx_amech_tenant ON auto_mechanic_profiles(tenant_id);

CREATE TABLE job_cards (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  workspace_id TEXT REFERENCES workspaces(id),
  tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  vehicle_plate TEXT NOT NULL,
  customer_phone TEXT NOT NULL,
  complaint TEXT NOT NULL,
  diagnosis TEXT,
  mechanic_id TEXT,
  labour_cost_kobo INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  parts_cost_kobo INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'open',
  created_at INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch()),
  updated_at INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
);
CREATE INDEX idx_jobcards_tenant ON job_cards(tenant_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_jobcards_status ON job_cards(status);

CREATE TABLE mechanic_parts_inventory (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  workspace_id TEXT REFERENCES workspaces(id),
  tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  part_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  part_number TEXT,
  quantity_in_stock INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  unit_cost_kobo INTEGER NOT NULL,
  reorder_level INTEGER DEFAULT 5,
  supplier TEXT,
  created_at INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
);
CREATE INDEX idx_mechparts_tenant ON mechanic_parts_inventory(tenant_id);
```
- All monetary values in kobo (P9)
- `tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL` on every table (T3)

**3b. Package implementation:**

Create `packages/verticals-auto-mechanic/src/index.ts`:
- Export: `registerAutoMechanicVertical(): VerticalRegistration`
- FSM states: `seeded` → `claimed` → `cac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- Transition guards: CAC verification check before `cac_verified`; VIO registration number required before `active`
- KYC tier guard: `requireKYCTier('tier_2')` for invoicing above threshold
- AI integration: call `packages/superagent` burn engine with `aiRights` check + NDPR consent gate (P10) + USSD exclusion (P12)
- No raw customer PII passed to AI — aggregate job card stats and parts consumption rates only (P13)
- `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`

**3c. API routes:**

Create `apps/api/src/routes/verticals/auto-mechanic.ts`:
- `POST /v1/verticals/auto-mechanic/register` — create workshop profile
- `GET /v1/verticals/auto-mechanic/:id` — fetch profile (tenant-scoped T3)
- `POST /v1/verticals/auto-mechanic/:id/transition` — FSM state change
- `POST /v1/verticals/auto-mechanic/:id/job-cards` — create job card
- `GET /v1/verticals/auto-mechanic/:id/job-cards` — list job cards (filterable by status)
- `POST /v1/verticals/auto-mechanic/:id/parts` — add/update parts inventory
- `POST /v1/verticals/auto-mechanic/:id/ai-advisory` — demand forecast + reorder alerts (gated by `aiRights` + NDPR consent P10; blocked on USSD P12)
- Mount in `apps/api/src/index.ts` under the verticals router

**3d. Tenant isolation (T3 — non-negotiable):**
- All D1 queries include `WHERE tenant_id = ?` bound to the authenticated tenant
- No cross-tenant data accessible via any route

---

**4. QA and verification:**

Act as a **Senior QA Engineer** with automotive services and fintech testing experience.

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-auto-mechanic/src/auto-mechanic.test.ts`

Positive test cases:
- Workshop profile registers successfully with valid workshop_name, state, LGA
- FSM transition from `seeded` to `claimed` succeeds
- FSM transition to `cac_verified` succeeds with valid CAC number provided
- Job card created with vehicle plate, complaint, integer labour_cost_kobo (P9)
- Parts inventory item added with integer unit_cost_kobo (P9)
- Parts reorder alert triggers when quantity_in_stock falls below reorder_level
- AI advisory returns demand forecast when `aiRights: true` and NDPR consent present
- Job card status updates from `open` to `in_progress` to `completed`

Negative / edge cases:
- Registration rejected without workshop_name
- FSM transition to invalid state returns typed error (T4)
- Labour cost with fractional kobo value rejected (P9)
- Cross-tenant job card access attempt returns 403 (T3)
- AI advisory blocked when `aiRights: false`
- AI advisory blocked when NDPR consent absent (P10)
- AI advisory on USSD session blocked (P12)
- Transition to `active` without VIO registration returns typed error

Security tests:
- Unauthenticated requests to all routes → 401
- Insufficient KYC tier for high-value invoice → 403

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push to GitHub:**

```bash
git add .
git commit -m "feat(vertical/auto-mechanic): Auto Mechanic vertical — job cards, parts inventory, AI demand forecast (M9)"
git push origin feat/vertical-auto-mechanic
```

PR description must reference:
- Verticals master plan entry for `auto-mechanic` (Commerce P2)
- Platform invariants enforced: P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4
- AI integration framework: DEMAND_PLANNING + SALES_FORECAST capabilities
- Migration number and tables created: `auto_mechanic_profiles`, `job_cards`, `mechanic_parts_inventory`

PR checklist:
- [ ] Vertical registered in FSM via `packages/verticals/src/`
- [ ] Migration created and numbered correctly (no gap, no collision)
- [ ] `primary_pillars` set to `["ops","branding","marketplace"]` in `VerticalRegistration`
- [ ] PR labeled with `3in1:pillar-1`, `3in1:pillar-2`, `3in1:pillar-3`
- [ ] All routes protected by correct KYC tier guard
- [ ] AI routes use SuperAgent burn engine (SuperAgent is cross-cutting, not a pillar)
- [ ] Cross-tenant isolation enforced on all D1 queries (T3)
- [ ] Tests ≥ 15, all passing
- [ ] Zero typecheck errors (`pnpm -r typecheck`)
- [ ] Zero lint errors (`pnpm -r lint`)

## TASK V-COMM-EXT-A2: Bakery / Confectionery Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `bakery`
- **Priority:** P2-Top100 — Bread, cakes, and confectionery are daily essentials across all Nigerian cities; over 30,000 registered bakeries (NAFDAC figures)
- **Milestone target:** M9

  - Entitlement model: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/entitlement-model.md

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in food production management software and multi-tenant SaaS platforms, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian food production sector — artisan bakeries, large-scale bread factories, pastry shops; Lagos/Kano/Ibadan market context
- NAFDAC food production licence and food handler certification compliance workflows
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode
- WebWaka SuperAgent integration — all AI features route through `packages/superagent`
- Verticals FSM lifecycle engine — extend base FSM, never replace it
- Nigerian regulatory context: NAFDAC (food production license, product registration), SON (product standards), state ministry of agriculture

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — Commerce verticals; entry for `bakery`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md` — Pillar 1/2/3 classification
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4
- `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv` — row for `bakery`
- `packages/verticals/src/` — FSM engine API
- `packages/auth/src/guards.ts` — KYC tier guards
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md` — SuperAgent WakaCU burn pattern
- `docs/governance/entitlement-model.md` — plan tier gating
- `infra/db/migrations/` — verify next unused migration number

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- NAFDAC bakery/food production licensing requirements — number of licensed bakeries in Nigeria, registration process, food handler card requirement per employee
- Top bakery management software used in Nigeria and West Africa (BakePOS, BakeBook, local WhatsApp-based order taking, manual)
- Key features demanded by Nigerian bakery operators — daily production planning, ingredient stock, custom cake orders with deposit, delivery scheduling, NAFDAC compliance tracking, daily sales summary
- Common pain points: no production schedule, raw material spoilage from over-ordering, no deposit system for custom cake orders

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give every Nigerian bakery a digital production planner, ingredient stock tracker, custom order pipeline, and NAFDAC compliance record — all integrated with Pillar 2 branded storefront for online cake orders
- **Schema additions:**
  - `bakery_profiles` — bakery name, NAFDAC number, production_license_expiry, CAC number, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `bakery_products` — product name, category (bread/cake/pastry/snack), unit_price_kobo, production_cost_kobo, daily_capacity, tenant_id
  - `bakery_orders` — customer_phone, product_id, quantity, customization_notes, deposit_kobo, balance_kobo, delivery_date, status (pending/confirmed/baking/ready/delivered), tenant_id
  - `bakery_ingredients` — ingredient name, unit (kg/litre/piece), quantity_in_stock, unit_cost_kobo, reorder_level, tenant_id
- **API routes:** register, profile GET, transition, products CRUD, orders CRUD, ingredients CRUD, ai-advisory
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `nafdac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent integration:** `DEMAND_PLANNING` (production volume forecasting), `SALES_FORECAST` — L2 autonomy; Growth plan minimum
- **Regulatory hook:** NAFDAC license number + expiry captured at `nafdac_verified`; food handler count stored on profile
- **KYC tier:** Tier 1 for basic order taking; Tier 2 for delivery fee collection above ₦50,000/day

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-bakery` from `main`.

**3a. Schema migration:**
Create `infra/db/migrations/[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_bakery.sql` with tables: `bakery_profiles`, `bakery_products`, `bakery_orders`, `bakery_ingredients`. All monetary columns in kobo (P9). `tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL` on all tables (T3). Appropriate indexes on `tenant_id` and `status`.

**3b. Package implementation:**
Create `packages/verticals-bakery/src/index.ts`:
- `registerBakeryVertical(): VerticalRegistration` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`
- FSM: `seeded` → `claimed` → `nafdac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- Transition guard: NAFDAC license number required for `nafdac_verified`; expiry date must be in future for `active`
- AI: pass only aggregate production/sales stats to SuperAgent — never customer PII or order details (P13)
- USSD exclusion at AI routes (P12)

**3c. API routes:**
Create `apps/api/src/routes/verticals/bakery.ts` with full CRUD for bakery profiles, products, orders, and ingredients. AI advisory route gated by `requireAiRights` + NDPR consent check. Mount under verticals router in `apps/api/src/index.ts`.

**3d. Tenant isolation:** `WHERE tenant_id = ?` on every D1 query (T3).

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-bakery/src/bakery.test.ts`

Positive:
- Bakery profile registers with valid data
- FSM transition `seeded` → `claimed` succeeds
- `nafdac_verified` transition succeeds with valid NAFDAC number and future expiry date
- Custom cake order created with integer deposit_kobo and balance_kobo (P9)
- Ingredient stock updated with integer unit_cost_kobo (P9)
- Ingredient triggers low-stock alert when below reorder_level
- AI demand forecast returns when `aiRights: true` and NDPR consent present

Negative:
- Registration fails without bakery_name
- `nafdac_verified` transition rejected with expired NAFDAC license
- Order with fractional kobo deposit rejected (P9)
- Cross-tenant order access → 403 (T3)
- AI advisory blocked: `aiRights: false`
- AI advisory blocked: NDPR consent absent (P10)
- AI advisory blocked: USSD session (P12)
- Invalid FSM state transition returns typed error (T4)

Security:
- Unauthenticated requests → 401
- Insufficient KYC tier → 403

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

## TASK V-COMM-EXT-A3: Beauty Salon / Barber Shop Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `beauty-salon`
- **Priority:** P2-Top100 — One of the highest-density small business categories in Nigeria; estimated 500,000+ salons and barbing salons nationwide
- **Milestone target:** M9
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 2 (Branding) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","branding","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:**
  - Verticals master plan: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md
  - 3-in-1 platform architecture: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md
  - Verticals FSM engine: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/packages/verticals/src/
  - Platform invariants: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/platform-invariants.md
  - Entitlement model: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/entitlement-model.md
  - AI integration framework: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md
  - SuperAgent product spec: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/superagent/02-product-spec.md

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in salon and personal care management software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian salon and barber sector — hair braiding, locs, weaves, barbing; Lagos/Abuja pricing norms, staff tipping culture
- NASC (Nigerian Association of Specialist Cosmetologists) and state-level business permit workflows
- Appointment booking and walk-in queue management for Nigerian market
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode
- WebWaka SuperAgent integration
- Verticals FSM lifecycle engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — entry for `beauty-salon`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4
- `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv` — row for `beauty-salon`
- `packages/verticals/src/` — FSM engine
- `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`
- `infra/db/migrations/` — next migration number

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian salon market — number of salons, average daily revenue, gender split (female hair salons vs barbing salons), major cities density
- Competitor analysis: Booksy Nigeria, Fresha Nigeria, Treatwell, local WhatsApp appointment management, WellaStore Nigeria
- Most-demanded features: appointment booking (WhatsApp link or web), staff scheduling, product/retail stock, client history (previous styles), loyalty points, daily revenue summary
- NASC membership requirements, state cosmetology regulations, local government health permit
- Pain points: no-show clients, staff lateness, cash handling disputes, no loyalty system

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give every Nigerian salon/barber shop digital appointment booking, staff scheduling, product stock tracking, and client history — with AI-powered slot demand forecasting
- **Schema:**
  - `beauty_salon_profiles` — salon_name, type (salon/barber/unisex), NASC_number, state_permit_number, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `salon_services` — service_name, duration_minutes, price_kobo, staff_id, tenant_id
  - `salon_appointments` — client_phone, service_id, staff_id, appointment_time (unix), status (booked/confirmed/in_service/completed/no_show/cancelled), deposit_kobo, tenant_id
  - `salon_products` — product_name, brand, unit_price_kobo, quantity_in_stock, tenant_id
- **API routes:** register, profile GET, transition, services CRUD, appointments CRUD, products CRUD, ai-advisory
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `permit_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `CUSTOMER_SEGMENTATION` (loyal vs lapsed clients), `DEMAND_PLANNING` (peak slot forecasting) — L2; Growth plan
- **Regulatory hook:** State business permit number captured at `permit_verified`
- **KYC:** Tier 1 sufficient for appointment deposits up to ₦50,000; Tier 2 for product retail sales above threshold

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-beauty-salon` from `main`.

**3a. Schema migration:** `infra/db/migrations/[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_beauty_salon.sql` — tables: `beauty_salon_profiles`, `salon_services`, `salon_appointments`, `salon_products`. All price fields in kobo (P9). `tenant_id NOT NULL` on all tables (T3).

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-beauty-salon/src/index.ts` — `registerBeautySalonVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. FSM: `seeded` → `claimed` → `permit_verified` → `active` → `suspended`. AI: aggregate booking stats only — no client names or phone numbers to AI layer (P13).

**3c. API routes:** `apps/api/src/routes/verticals/beauty-salon.ts`. All routes `tenant_id`-scoped. AI advisory blocked on USSD (P12).

**3d. Tenant isolation:** T3 on all queries.

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-beauty-salon/src/beauty-salon.test.ts`

Positive:
- Salon profile registers with name, type, state
- FSM `seeded` → `claimed` → `permit_verified` with valid permit number
- Appointment created with integer deposit_kobo (P9)
- Appointment status moves: `booked` → `confirmed` → `in_service` → `completed`
- Product added with integer unit_price_kobo (P9)
- AI peak-slot forecast returns when `aiRights: true` + NDPR consent

Negative:
- Registration fails without salon_name
- Appointment deposit with fractional kobo rejected (P9)
- Cross-tenant appointment access → 403 (T3)
- AI blocked: `aiRights: false`
- AI blocked: NDPR absent (P10)
- AI blocked: USSD session (P12)
- Invalid FSM transition → typed error (T4)
- `permit_verified` without permit number → typed error

Security:
- Unauthenticated → 401
- Wrong KYC tier → 403

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/beauty-salon): Beauty Salon/Barber vertical — appointments, staff scheduling, AI slot forecast (M9)"
git push origin feat/vertical-beauty-salon
```
PR checklist: FSM registered, migration correct, `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`, labeled `3in1:pillar-1/2/3`, all guards correct, SuperAgent burn engine used, T3 enforced, ≥15 tests passing, zero typecheck/lint errors.

## TASK V-COMM-EXT-A4: Bookshop / Stationery Store Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `bookshop`
- **Priority:** P2-Top100 — Ubiquitous across school zones, university towns, markets; critical for educational material distribution
- **Milestone target:** M9
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:**
  - Verticals master plan: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md
  - 3-in-1 platform architecture: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md

  - Platform invariants: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/platform-invariants.md
  - Entitlement model: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/entitlement-model.md
  - AI integration framework: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md
  - SuperAgent product spec: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/superagent/02-product-spec.md

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in retail inventory and education commerce, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian book retail market — WAEC/NECO prescribed textbook cycles, university reading lists, religious book demand, Ojota/Onitsha book markets
- CAC business registration requirements for retail shops
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode
- WebWaka SuperAgent integration
- Verticals FSM lifecycle engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — entry for `bookshop`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4
- `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv` — row for `bookshop`
- `packages/verticals/src/` — FSM engine
- `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`
- `infra/db/migrations/` — next migration number

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian book market size — estimated retail value, WAEC/NECO textbook demand cycles (January/September peaks), university reading list bulk orders
- Competitors: Roving Heights, Quintessence Books, Laterna Ventures, CSS Bookshops, University Press PLC, Amazon.ng
- Key features: ISBN-based catalogue, bulk school order management, layaway/school-fee instalment for book sets, low-stock alerts, supplier (Ibadan University Press, NERDC) purchase orders
- CAC registration requirements for retail book business, state business premises permit

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian bookshops digital inventory with ISBN catalogue, school bulk order management, supplier tracking, and AI-powered seasonal demand forecasting
- **Schema:**
  - `bookshop_profiles` — shop_name, CAC_number, state, LGA, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `bookshop_catalogue` — isbn, title, author, publisher, category (textbook/novel/religious/stationery), unit_price_kobo, quantity_in_stock, tenant_id
  - `bookshop_orders` — customer_phone, order_items (JSON), total_kobo, payment_status, delivery_method (pickup/delivery), tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `cac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `DEMAND_PLANNING` (seasonal textbook demand) + `SALES_FORECAST` — L2; Growth plan
- **Regulatory hook:** CAC number at `cac_verified`
- **KYC:** Tier 1 for retail sales; Tier 2 for bulk school orders above ₦200,000

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-bookshop` from `main`.

**3a. Schema:** `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_bookshop.sql` — `bookshop_profiles`, `bookshop_catalogue`, `bookshop_orders`. All monetary fields in kobo (P9). `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3).

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-bookshop/src/index.ts` — `registerBookshopVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. FSM: `seeded` → `claimed` → `cac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`. AI: aggregate sales by category only — no customer PII (P13).

**3c. Routes:** `apps/api/src/routes/verticals/bookshop.ts`. AI advisory blocked on USSD (P12).

**3d. Tenant isolation:** T3 on all queries.

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-bookshop/src/bookshop.test.ts`

Positive: register with valid data; FSM transitions succeed; catalogue item with integer unit_price_kobo; order created with integer total_kobo; seasonal forecast AI returns when aiRights + NDPR consent present. Negative: fractional kobo rejected (P9); cross-tenant access → 403 (T3); AI blocked: aiRights false, NDPR absent, USSD session; invalid FSM → typed error (T4). Security: unauth → 401; wrong KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/bookshop): Bookshop vertical — ISBN catalogue, school bulk orders, AI demand forecast (M9)"
git push origin feat/vertical-bookshop
```
PR checklist: FSM, migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`, labeled `3in1:pillar-1/3`, guards, SuperAgent, T3, ≥15 tests, zero typecheck/lint.

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in event catering and food service management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian catering and events industry — owambe (social parties), aso-ebi events, corporate catering, Lagos Island vs mainland pricing dynamics
- NAFDAC food handler certification and local government event permit requirements
- Menu costing, portion planning, head-count-based pricing in kobo
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode
- WebWaka SuperAgent integration
- Verticals FSM lifecycle engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — entry for `catering`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4

- `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian catering market size — number of caterers, average event catering contract value (head count × price per head), seasonal peaks (December, Easter, Sallah)
- Competitor catering management tools: Caterease, HoneyBook (US but used in NG), WhatsApp-based manual quote management
- Key features: event quote builder (per-head pricing in kobo), menu customization, deposit and balance payment tracking, ingredient prep list, staff allocation, delivery logistics, NAFDAC food handler cards per staff member
- NAFDAC food handler certification for mobile caterers, local government event catering permit

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give every Nigerian caterer a digital event pipeline — quotes in kobo, deposit tracking, menu management, staff allocation, and AI demand forecasting for peak seasons
- **Schema:**
  - `catering_profiles` — business_name, NAFDAC_cert, CAC_number, speciality (Nigerian/continental/confectionery/all), workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `catering_events` — client_phone, event_type, event_date, guest_count, price_per_head_kobo, total_kobo, deposit_kobo, balance_kobo, status (quoted/confirmed/prepping/delivered/settled), tenant_id
  - `catering_menus` — menu_name, description, cost_per_head_kobo, tenant_id
  - `catering_staff` — staff_name, role (cook/server/driver), nafdac_card_number, tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `nafdac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `DEMAND_PLANNING` (seasonal event peak forecasting), `SALES_FORECAST` — L2; Growth plan
- **Regulatory hook:** NAFDAC food handler cert count and CAC number at `nafdac_verified`
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for events above ₦500,000 total contract value

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-catering` from `main`.

**3a. Schema:** `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_catering.sql` — all monetary fields in kobo (P9), `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3).

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-catering/src/index.ts` — `registerCateringVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. No client PII or event details to AI layer (P13) — aggregate event counts and revenue totals only.

**3c. Routes:** `apps/api/src/routes/verticals/catering.ts`. AI advisory blocked on USSD (P12).

**3d. T3:** `WHERE tenant_id = ?` on all D1 queries.

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-catering/src/catering.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM transitions including `nafdac_verified` with valid cert; event quote with integer price_per_head_kobo, deposit_kobo (P9); event status progression; AI forecast with aiRights + NDPR consent. Negative: fractional kobo (P9); cross-tenant → 403 (T3); AI blocked triply (aiRights, NDPR, USSD); invalid FSM → typed error (T4); `nafdac_verified` without cert → typed error. Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/catering): Catering vertical — event pipeline, NAFDAC compliance, AI seasonal forecast (M9)"
git push origin feat/vertical-catering
```

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in electronics repair shop management software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian electronics repair sector — Computer Village Lagos cluster (10,000+ shops), Onitsha electronics market, Aba tech enclave; device repair workflow
- SON (Standards Organisation of Nigeria) product compliance, CAC registration for electronics dealers
- Device intake logging, fault diagnosis, parts procurement, repair status tracking
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode
- WebWaka SuperAgent integration
- Verticals FSM lifecycle engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — entry for `electronics-repair`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4

- `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian electronics repair market — Computer Village Lagos daily revenue, NCC device market statistics, popular devices repaired (iPhones, Samsung, Tecno, Infinix, laptops)
- Competitors: no major dedicated repair shop SaaS in Nigeria; management via WhatsApp, handwritten job cards, Jiji for parts sourcing
- Key features: device intake form (device type, IMEI, fault description, customer phone), repair job tracking, spare parts inventory (sourced from Computer Village suppliers), repair cost quotation in kobo, customer SMS notification on completion, warranty tracking
- SON product standards for refurbished devices, CAC registration for electronics dealers

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Digitise the job card and parts workflow for Nigerian electronics repair shops — intake, diagnosis, parts sourcing, repair tracking, and customer notification
- **Schema:**
  - `electronics_repair_profiles` — shop_name, CAC_number, SON_registration, location_cluster (computer_village/onitsha/aba/other), workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `repair_jobs` — device_type, brand, model, imei, fault_description, customer_phone, diagnosis, labour_cost_kobo, parts_cost_kobo, status (intake/diagnosing/awaiting_parts/repairing/completed/collected), warranty_days, tenant_id
  - `repair_parts_inventory` — part_name, compatible_models (JSON), quantity, unit_cost_kobo, supplier, tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `cac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `DEMAND_PLANNING` (parts demand by device brand/model), `SALES_FORECAST` — L2; Growth plan
- **Regulatory hook:** CAC number + SON registration at `cac_verified`
- **KYC:** Tier 1 for repair invoicing; Tier 2 for device resale above ₦100,000

---

**3a. Schema:** `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_electronics_repair.sql`. All monetary in kobo (P9). `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3). Index on `status`, `tenant_id`.

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-electronics-repair/src/index.ts` — `registerElectronicsRepairVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. Parts demand AI: aggregate part names + sales counts only — no IMEI, customer phone (P13).

**3c. Routes:** `apps/api/src/routes/verticals/electronics-repair.ts`. AI blocked on USSD (P12).

**3d. T3:** All D1 queries scoped to `tenant_id`.

Negative: fractional kobo (P9); cross-tenant → 403 (T3); AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error (T4). Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

  - AI integration framework: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md
  - SuperAgent product spec: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/superagent/02-product-spec.md

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in florist and perishable goods management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian florist market — Lagos Island high-end florists, market flower vendors in Mile 12 / Oyingbo, event decoration sector (church, wedding, burial); seasonal demand patterns (Valentine's, Christmas, New Year, Sallah)
- CAC business registration, state agriculture department awareness for plant importation
- Perishable inventory management — stock expiry, daily procurement from Mile 12 market
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode
- WebWaka SuperAgent integration
- Verticals FSM lifecycle engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — entry for `florist`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4
- `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv` — row for `florist`
- `packages/verticals/src/` — FSM engine
- `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`
- `infra/db/migrations/` — next migration number

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian florist market — number of registered florists vs informal vendors, average wedding decoration contract value, funeral flower demand (tied to church density)
- Competitors: FloralNaija, Stacy Floral, Petalz NG, event decoration firms; mostly WhatsApp-order based
- Key features: arrangement catalogue with images + prices in kobo, event order booking (wedding/funeral/corporate), perishable stock tracking (days remaining), daily procurement records, delivery scheduling, Pillar 2 online catalogue
- CAC registration, state ministry of agriculture awareness for imported plants

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian florists a digital arrangement catalogue, event order pipeline, perishable stock tracker, and AI seasonal demand forecaster — with Pillar 2 branded online shop
- **Schema:**
  - `florist_profiles` — business_name, CAC_number, speciality (wedding/funeral/corporate/retail), workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `florist_arrangements` — name, description, occasion (wedding/funeral/birthday/corporate), price_kobo, image_url, tenant_id
  - `florist_orders` — client_phone, arrangement_id, event_date, delivery_address, deposit_kobo, balance_kobo, status (enquiry/confirmed/preparing/delivered/settled), tenant_id
  - `florist_stock` — flower_name, quantity_in_stock, unit_cost_kobo, expiry_date (unix), supplier_name, tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `cac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `DEMAND_PLANNING` (seasonal occasion peaks) + `SALES_FORECAST` — L2; Growth plan
- **Regulatory hook:** CAC number at `cac_verified`
- **KYC:** Tier 1 for retail; Tier 2 for event contracts above ₦300,000

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-florist` from `main`.

**3a. Schema:** `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_florist.sql`. All monetary in kobo (P9). `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3). Index on `expiry_date` for stock expiry alerts.

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-florist/src/index.ts` — `registerFloristVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. AI: aggregate sales by occasion only — no client details (P13).

**3c. Routes:** `apps/api/src/routes/verticals/florist.ts`. AI blocked on USSD (P12).

**3d. T3:** All queries scoped.

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-florist/src/florist.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM transitions; arrangement with integer price_kobo; order with integer deposit_kobo, balance_kobo (P9); stock expiry warning at threshold; AI seasonal forecast with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: fractional kobo; cross-tenant → 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error (T4). Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/florist): Florist vertical — arrangement catalogue, event orders, AI seasonal forecast (M9)"
git push origin feat/vertical-florist
```
PR checklist: FSM, migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`, labeled, guards, SuperAgent, T3, ≥15 tests, zero errors.

## TASK V-COMM-EXT-A9: Food Vendor / Street Food Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `food-vendor`
- **Priority:** P2-Top100 — Nigeria's street food economy is enormous; estimated millions of food vendors (mama-put, buka, suya spots, shawarma stands, bole spots) operating informally
- **Milestone target:** M9
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:**
  - Verticals master plan: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md
  - 3-in-1 platform architecture: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md
  - Verticals FSM engine: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/packages/verticals/src/
  - Platform invariants: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/platform-invariants.md
  - Entitlement model: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/entitlement-model.md
  - AI integration framework: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md
  - SuperAgent product spec: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/superagent/02-product-spec.md

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in informal food service and street vendor digitisation, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian street food economy — mama-put (roadside canteen), buka (local restaurant), suya, shawarma, bole/plantain spots; cash-only dynamics, USSD and feature phone operators
- Local government food vendor permit, NAFDAC awareness for packaged food sold alongside cooked meals
- Informal sector onboarding — Tier 1 KYC sufficient; simple FSM (3 states); no mandatory CAC
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode
- WebWaka SuperAgent integration
- Verticals FSM lifecycle engine — simplified 3-state FSM for P2 informal verticals

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — entry for `food-vendor`; informal sector notes
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4
- `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv` — row for `food-vendor`
- `packages/verticals/src/` — FSM engine (3-state simplified pattern)
- `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`
- `infra/db/migrations/` — next migration number

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigeria street food economy — estimated number of vendors, average daily turnover per vendor, role of street food in urban food security (Lagos, Kano, Onitsha, Port Harcourt)
- Digital tools used by street vendors today — mostly none; some use WhatsApp catalogue; Chowdeck and Glovo for delivery but require digital infrastructure
- Key features for street food vendors: simple daily sales tally, item price list (menu) in kobo, low-data/USSD-friendly design, customer discovery via Pillar 3 directory, local government permit tracking
- LGA food vendor permit, NAFDAC food handler awareness

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian street food vendors a simple digital menu, daily sales tracker, and Pillar 3 discovery profile — lightweight enough for USSD and feature phone users
- **Schema:**
  - `food_vendor_profiles` — vendor_name, food_type (mama_put/buka/suya/shawarma/bole/other), location_description, lga, state, lg_permit_number, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `food_vendor_menu` — item_name, price_kobo, available (boolean), tenant_id
  - `food_vendor_sales` — sale_date (unix date only), total_kobo, items_sold_count, tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `active` (3-state informal pattern — no mandatory regulatory verification state)
- **SuperAgent:** `SALES_FORECAST` (daily revenue trend) — L1 autonomy (informational only); Growth plan; NDPR consent required
- **Regulatory hook:** LG permit number optional at `claimed`; stored but not a blocking gate
- **KYC:** Tier 1 sufficient — street food vendors are squarely the informal economy base

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-food-vendor` from `main`.

**3a. Schema:** `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_food_vendor.sql`. All monetary in kobo (P9). `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3). Simple 3-table design keeping the informal-sector principle (minimal friction).

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-food-vendor/src/index.ts` — `registerFoodVendorVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. FSM: `seeded` → `claimed` → `active`. AI: aggregate daily revenue totals only — no vendor location or personal data (P13). L1 autonomy cap (informational only).

**3c. Routes:** `apps/api/src/routes/verticals/food-vendor.ts`. AI blocked on USSD (P12) — especially important here given USSD usage by this demographic.

**3d. T3:** All queries scoped to `tenant_id`.

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-food-vendor/src/food-vendor.test.ts`

Positive: register with vendor_name + food_type + state; FSM `seeded` → `claimed` → `active`; menu item with integer price_kobo (P9); daily sales entry with integer total_kobo (P9); AI revenue trend returns with aiRights + NDPR consent (L1 informational). Negative: fractional kobo (P9); cross-tenant → 403 (T3); AI blocked: aiRights false, NDPR absent, USSD session (P12); invalid FSM → typed error (T4). Security: unauth → 401.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/food-vendor): Food Vendor vertical — digital menu, daily sales, Pillar 3 discovery (M9)"
git push origin feat/vertical-food-vendor
```
PR checklist:
- [ ] Vertical registered in FSM (3-state informal pattern)
- [ ] Migration created correctly
- [ ] `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`
- [ ] PR labeled: `3in1:pillar-1`, `3in1:pillar-3`
- [ ] Tier 1 KYC gate (informal sector)
- [ ] AI routes use SuperAgent burn engine at L1 autonomy
- [ ] USSD exclusion explicitly tested (P12)
- [ ] T3 enforced on all D1 queries
- [ ] Tests ≥ 15, all passing
- [ ] Zero typecheck errors (`pnpm -r typecheck`)
- [ ] Zero lint errors (`pnpm -r lint`)
